text: Bundles four separate bets (data collection, session analysis, automated filing, deploy gates) as one architecture without proving they're interdependent
Session model is a load-bearing assumption already known to be broken for HTTP/Hugin — invalidates Layer 2 methodology
Does not prove incremental value over existing retrieval analytics — overstates 'no visibility' when 7 of 22 tools already have observability
Scale and hot-path cost model lacks numbers — assertions without measurements for rows/day, bytes/row, latency impact, SD-card write amplification
Coupling from universal handler wrapping is broader than admitted — assumes tools remain synchronous request/response forever
Degradation behavior unspecified — what happens when INSERT fails, when cron fails, when gh is unauthenticated
Anti-pattern heuristics are unsupported — every proposed pattern has legitimate counter-interpretations, no validation plan, no precision targets
Operational burden materially understated — full proposal is a new subsystem plus recurring governance loop, not lightweight telemetry
Opportunity cost cuts against the proposal — known bugs and benchmark work are more defensible than meta-tooling
No success criteria or exit criteria defined — 'autonomous product improvement' is a vision not a measurable goal
Reversibility is more expensive than 'drop the table' — automated issues, weekly reports, and deploy gates create workflow coupling that requires socio-technical rollback
